commands.review_summary:
The PR persists accepted CLI user turns before the CLI backend attempt, carries a persisted-user signal into post-run transcript mirroring, and suppresses duplicate user persistence across direct and queued fallbacks.

PR surface: Source +59, Tests +143. Total +202 across 11 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current main writes the CLI user turn only in the post-run transcript mirror, so a thrown CLI attempt can skip the append. I did not run a live failure in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Human review should wait on contributor-provided real behavior proof; I did not find a narrow code defect for ClawSweeper to repair.

target.title: fix(agents): persist CLI user turns before attempts
target.body:
## Summary
- Persist accepted CLI user turns to the session transcript after `before_agent_run` hooks pass and before the backend attempt or nonblocking `llm_input` notification can fail.
- Carry the persisted-user signal through post-run transcript mirroring so successful CLI turns append only the assistant and do not duplicate the same user message.
- Thread the same signal through direct and queued fallback dispatch so a later fallback candidate does not persist the same inbound twice.

Fixes #86592

## Verification
- `pnpm test src/agents/cli-runner.reliability.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/commands/agent.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm format:check src/agents/agent-command.ts src/agents/cli-runner.reliability.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner.ts src/agents/cli-runner/types.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.ts src/commands/agent.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/agents/agent-command.ts src/agents/cli-runner.reliability.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner.ts src/agents/cli-runner/types.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.ts src/commands/agent.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof
content: behavior

environment: local source checkout, Node 22.22.2, pnpm 11.2.2, temp session transcript files on the real filesystem.

steps:
1. Ran the CLI runner with a real session JSONL file and a backend process that exits before returning an assistant result.
2. Verified the accepted CLI user turn is appended before the backend failure is surfaced.
3. Ran the post-run CLI transcript mirror with the user turn already persisted and verified only the assistant is appended.
4. Ran direct and queued fallback tests where a CLI candidate persists the user turn and then fails, followed by another candidate.

evidence:
- `src/agents/cli-runner.reliability.test.ts`: user line is durable before the backend failure and the persisted-user callback fires.
- `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts`: assistant-only mirror after a pre-persisted user turn.
- `src/commands/agent.test.ts`: post-run CLI transcript mirroring receives `skipUserMessage: true` after the runner persisted the user turn.
- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts` and `src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts`: CLI-to-fallback dispatch suppresses duplicate user persistence after the first candidate reports the user turn persisted.

observedResult: the accepted CLI user turn remains in the session transcript when the backend attempt fails, successful post-run mirroring does not duplicate it, and fallback candidates reuse the persisted-user signal instead of writing the same inbound twice.

notTested: full live Telegram topic E2E was not run locally. The focused proof covers the shared CLI runner, direct agent command, queued follow-up runner, fallback suppression, and real session JSONL writes.
